message:
✨ Add DeploymentProgress card with real K8s deployment status (#18)

Backend:
- Add Deployment struct with rollout status (running/deploying/failed)
- Add GetDeployments method to MultiClusterClient
- Add /api/mcp/deployments endpoint

Frontend:
- Add useDeployments hook with polling
- Add DeploymentProgress card component with progress bars
- Register card in Dashboard component

The card shows:
- All deployments across clusters
- Rollout progress with visual progress bar
- Ready/total replica count
- Status indicators (running, deploying, failed)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+export interface Deployment {
+  name: string
+  namespace: string
+  cluster?: string
+  status: 'running' | 'deploying' | 'failed'
+  replicas: number
+  readyReplicas: number
+  updatedReplicas: number

@@ -263,6 +277,39 @@ export function useDeploymentIssues(cluster?: string, namespace?: string) {
   return { issues, isLoading, error, refetch }
 }
 
+// Hook to get deployments with rollout status
+export function useDeployments(cluster?: string, namespace?: string) {
+  const [deployments, setDeployments] = useState<Deployment[]>([])
+  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true)
+  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
+
+  const refetch = useCallback(async () => {
+    setIsLoading(true)
+    try {
+      const params = new URLSearchParams()
+      if (cluster) params.append('cluster', cluster)
+      if (namespace) params.append('namespace', namespace)
+      const { data } = await api.get<{ deployments: Deployment[] }>(`/api/mcp/deployments?${params}`)
+      setDeployments(data.deployments || [])
+      setError(null)
+    } catch (err) {
+      setError('Failed to fetch deployments')
+      setDeployments(getDemoDeployments())
+    } finally {
+      setIsLoading(false)
+    }
+  }, [cluster, namespace])
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    refetch()
+    // Poll every 10 seconds for deployment updates
+    const interval = setInterval(refetch, 10000)
+    return () => clearInterval(interval)
+  }, [refetch])
+
+  return { deployments, isLoading, error, refetch }
+}
+
 // Hook to get warning events
 export function useWarningEvents(cluster?: string, namespace?: string, limit = 20) {
   const [events, setEvents] = useState<ClusterEvent[]>([])
